---
title: "Maximal model"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
source('../assets/setup.R')
```

The "maximal model" is the version of the model that **contains all possible [random intercepts](rdint.html) and [random slopes](rdslp.html) that the data can support.**

A maximal model **does _not_ need to contain every possible [fixed effect](fixef.html).**
The term "maximal model" refers only to the [random effect](ranef.html) structure, not the fixed effects.
(As always, fixed effects should be chosen based on your RQ.)

For how to identify the maximal model for your data, see  [Identify possible random effects](identify-ranef.html).

If you try to fit the maximal model to your data, you might find that it fails to converge (see [Troubleshoot common issues](troubleshoot.html)).
If this is the case, you may need to [change the model's optimiser](optimiser.html) and/or [simplify](simplify.html) the random effect structure to a non-maximal version that does converge.


## Linked flash cards

### Outgoing links

- TODO


### Backlinks

- TODO
